mn131:1.2: that on one occasion the Blessed One was staying near Sāvatthī in Jeta’s Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s monastery.
mn131:1.5: “Yes, lord,” the monks responded to him.
mn131:2.1: “Monks, I will teach you the summary & exposition of one who has had an auspicious day.
mn131:2.2: Listen & pay close attention. I will speak.”
mn131:2.3: “As you say, lord,” the monks responded to him.
mn131:3.1: You shouldn’t chase after the past
mn131:3.2: or place expectations on the future.
mn131:3.8: you clearly see right there, right there.
mn131:3.10: that’s how you develop the heart.
mn131:3.11: Ardently doing what should be done today,
mn131:3.13: There is no bargaining with Mortality & his mighty horde.
mn131:3.14: Whoever lives thus ardently, relentlessly both day & night,
mn131:3.15: has truly had an auspicious day:
mn131:4.1: “And how, monks, does one chase after the past?
mn131:4.2: One gets carried away with the delight of ‘In the past I had such a form [body]’ … ‘In the past I had such a feeling’ … ‘In the past I had such a perception’ … ‘In the past I had such a fabrication’ … ‘In the past I had such a consciousness.’
mn131:4.3: This is called chasing after the past.
mn131:5.1: “And how does one not chase after the past?
mn131:5.2: One does not get carried away with the delight of ‘In the past I had such a form’ … ‘In the past I had such a feeling’ … ‘In the past I had such a perception’ … ‘In the past I had such a fabrication’ … ‘In the past I had such a consciousness.’
mn131:5.3: This is called not chasing after the past.
mn131:6.1: “And how does one place expectations on the future?
mn131:6.2: One gets carried away with the delight of ‘In the future I might have such a form’ … ‘In the future I might have such a feeling’ … ‘In the future I might have such a perception’ … ‘In the future I might have such a fabrication’ … ‘In the future I might have such a consciousness.’
mn131:6.3: This is called placing expectations on the future.
mn131:7.1: “And how does one not place expectations on the future?
mn131:7.2: One does not get carried away with the delight of ‘In the future I might have such a form’ … ‘In the future I might have such a feeling’ … ‘In the future I might have such a perception’ … ‘In the future I might have such a fabrication’ … ‘In the future I might have such a consciousness.’
mn131:7.3: This is called not placing expectations on the future.
mn131:8.1: “And how is one taken in with regard to present qualities?
mn131:8.2: There is the case where an uninstructed run-of-the-mill person who has not seen the noble ones, is not versed in the teachings of the noble ones, is not trained in the teachings of the noble ones,
mn131:8.3: sees form as self, or self as possessing form, or form as in self, or self as in form.
mn131:8.4: “He/she sees feeling as self, or self as possessing feeling, or feeling as in self, or self as in feeling.
mn131:8.5: “He/she sees perception as self, or self as possessing perception, or perception as in self, or self as in perception.
mn131:8.6: “He/she sees fabrications as self, or self as possessing fabrications, or fabrications as in self, or self as in fabrications.
mn131:8.7: “He/she sees consciousness as self, or self as possessing consciousness, or consciousness as in self, or self as in consciousness.
mn131:8.8: This is called being taken in with regard to present qualities.
mn131:9.1: “And how is one not taken in with regard to present qualities?
mn131:9.2: There is the case where a disciple of the noble ones who has seen the noble ones, is versed in the teachings of the noble ones, is well-trained in the teachings of the noble ones,
mn131:9.3: does not see form as self, or self as possessing form, or form as in self, or self as in form.
mn131:9.4: “He/she does not see feeling as self, or self as possessing feeling, or feeling as in self, or self as in feeling.
mn131:9.5: “He/she does not see perception as self, or self as possessing perception, or perception as in self, or self as in perception.
mn131:9.6: “He/she does not see fabrications as self, or self as possessing fabrications, or fabrications as in self, or self as in fabrications.
mn131:9.7: “He/she does not see consciousness as self, or self as possessing consciousness, or consciousness as in self, or self as in consciousness.
mn131:9.8: This is called not being taken in with regard to present qualities.
mn131:10.1: You shouldn’t chase after the past
mn131:10.2: or place expectations on the future.
mn131:10.8: you clearly see right there, right there.
mn131:10.10: that’s how you develop the heart.
mn131:10.11: Ardently doing what should be done today,
mn131:10.13: There is no bargaining with Mortality & his mighty horde.
mn131:10.14: Whoever lives thus ardently, relentlessly both day & night,
mn131:10.15: has truly had an auspicious day:
mn131:11.1: “‘Monks, I will teach you the summary & exposition of one who has had an auspicious day’:
mn131:11.2: Thus it was said, and in reference to this was it said.”
mn131:11.3: That is what the Blessed One said.
mn131:11.4: Gratified, the monks delighted in the Blessed One’s words.
